title: Buffers for Chloroplast Isolation from Diatoms
author: P. Dreux Chappell
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ea2bage

steps.title: Prepare Isolation Buffer
steps.instruction: Isolation Buffer (40 mL) 1) Mix: 20 ml 1 M sorbitol 400 μl 0.6 M Na 2 -EDTA 200 μl 1 M MgCl 2 400 μl 1 M KCl 40 μl 1 M MnCl 2 2 ml 1 M HEPES-KOH pH = 8.0 16.96 ml Sterile H 2 O. 2) Add bovine serum albumen (BSA) 1% (w/v) just before use to subset of isolation buffer needed at the beginning of the procedure

steps.title: Prepare PEG-6000 Solution
steps.instruction: PEG-6000 Solution Mix: 6 g PEG 10 ml water

steps.title: Prepare Percoll Solution
steps.instruction: Percoll Solution (20 mL Stock) Mix: 19 ml Percoll 1 ml PEG – 6000 solution 0.2 g Ficoll 0.2 g BSA

steps.title: Prepare Gradient Mixture
steps.instruction: Gradient Mixture (10 mL Stock) Mix: 250 μl 1 M HEPES-KOH pH = 8.0 1 ml 0.1 M EDTA 6.26 ml 1 M sorbitol 2.49 ml water

steps.title: Prepare 3X Bottom Layer (80% Percoll)
steps.instruction: 3X Bottom Layer (80% Percoll) Mix: 5.7 ml Percoll Solution 1.01 ml Gradient Mixture

steps.title: Prepare 3X Top Layer (40% Percoll)
steps.instruction: 3X Top Layer (40% Percoll) Mix: 5.04 ml Percoll Solution 6.96 ml Gradient Mixture

steps.title: Prepare 10M Ammonium Fluoride (NH₄F) Solution
steps.instruction: 10M Ammonium Fluoride (NH 4 F) Mix: 3.7 g NH 4 F 10 ml H 2 O
